---
title: "CV"
pagetitle: "CV — Fabio I. Martinenghi"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
toc-location: right
toc-title: "Sections"
---

[**Download CV (PDF)**](assets/cv.pdf){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color:#777;font-size:0.9rem">Last updated: April 2026</span>

## Fields of Interest {.cv-section}

Health Economics · Education · Law & Economics.

## Employment {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-entry}
Oct 2024 –

**Research Fellow, [MandEval](https://www.uwa.edu.au/news/Article/2023/March/Ground-breaking-medical-research-projects-awarded-22-million)**\
University of Newcastle & University of Western Australia\
*Studying the impact of vaccine mandates on uptake using Australian administrative data (AIR-PLIDA).*
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
Sep 2021 – Sep 2024

**Research Associate, Economics of Education Knowledge Hub**\
University of New South Wales, Sydney\
*Worked on academic and non-academic projects using NSW Education administrative data.*
:::

## Other Positions & Affiliations {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-entry}
Apr 2024 –

**Honorary Associate Lecturer**\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
Aug 2023 –

**Affiliate, Tax and Transfer Policy Institute**\
Crawford School of Public Policy, Australian National University
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
May 2023 –

**Investigator, NHMRC grant APP1127437**\
National Perinatal Epidemiology and Statistics Unit · Centre for Big Data Research in Health, UNSW
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
Oct 2020 –

**Affiliate, ARC Centre of Excellence for Children and Families over the Life Course**\
Universities of Sydney, Melbourne, Queensland and Western Australia
:::

## Education {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-entry}
2018–2020

**Visiting Scholar, University of New South Wales**\
*Awarded Tuition Fee Scholarship.*
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2016–2020

**Ph.D. in Economics, University of Milan**\
Thesis: *Essays in Policy Evaluation*. Research topics: Applied Micro, Law & Economics.
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
Jan 2018

**IRVAPP Winter School — Advanced Methods in Impact Evaluation**\
*Topics: discontinuities, causality and machine learning, instrumental variables.*
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2014–2016

**MSc in Economics, University of Nottingham**
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2011–2014

**Undergraduate Degree (*Cum Laude*) in Philosophy, Università Cattolica del Sacro Cuore, Italy**\
Dissertation: *Measurement and Modelling in Economics: An Epistemological Perspective*.
:::

## Working Papers & Publications {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-paper}
**Legal aid eligibility and court outcomes: a design-based double-machine-learning approach.**\
*Forthcoming, Journal of Law & Economics.* [TTPI WP](https://crawford.anu.edu.au/sites/default/files/2025-02/complete_f_martinenghi_wp_feb_2024.pdf).\
*Double-machine-learning approach with administrative data on Legal Aid applicants to estimate the effect of aid refusal on court outcomes.*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**An open repository of COVID-19 vaccine mandate studies with a worked scoping review of quasi-experimental evidence.**\
Lead author, with Blyth, C. and the MandEval team. *Forthcoming, npj Vaccines.* [Article](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41541-026-01454-4).\
*A dataset covering more than 500 papers on COVID-19 mandates, manually curated and with data extracted by an LLM, plus a worked scoping review of the quasi-experimental evidence on uptake and downstream outcomes.*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**Vaccinating Now or Vaccinating Later: separating pull-forward and net effects using a dynamic regression discontinuity design.**\
Lead author, with Attwell, K., Genie, M. and the MandEval team. *Submitted.* [PDF](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hNJ9m_VlW7hmkQfRk0wGbvPBWwIEQkdn/view?usp=drive_link).\
*Studies a novel COVID-19 vaccine mandate targeting graduating high-school students. A daily panel of linked administrative data (AIR-PLIDA) lets RDD run dynamically, disentangling intensive and extensive margins. Develops a toy model for external validity.*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**Career, Family, and IVF: the impact of involuntary childlessness and fertility treatment.**\
With Maryam Naghsh Nejad. [IZA WP](https://www.iza.org/publications/dp/17965/career-family-and-ivf-the-impact-of-involuntary-childlessness-and-fertility-treatment).\
*Dynamic triple DID approach to study the impacts of childlessness and IVF on women's income and mental health, using Australian administrative data.*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**Conceiving naturally after IVF: the effect of an ART conception on obstetric outcomes.**\
With Zhang, X., Rombauts, L. and Chambers, G. [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.00234v2).\
*Leverages idiosyncratic spontaneous pregnancy after a failed ART cycle to estimate the effect of ART on outcomes such as c-section and birth weight, using Australian administrative data (ANZARD).*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**Getting Explicit Instruction Right.**\
With Richard Holden. *Submitted.* [arXiv](http://arxiv.org/abs/2506.10480).\
*Studies the dramatic effect of a public NSW school introducing the Explicit Instruction pedagogy, using synthetic control on administrative school data.*
:::

::: {.cv-paper}
**Grandmaternal health shocks, informal childcare, and maternal outcomes.**\
With Jan Kabatek. *In progress.*\
*Studies the effect of grandmaternal acute health episodes (identified by selected PBS drugs) on the labour outcomes of mothers with children aged 0–4.*
:::

## Teaching Experience {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-entry}
2021

**Lecturer-in-Charge, *Environmental Economics***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2021

**Teaching Assistant, *Business Decision Making***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2021

**Teaching Assistant, *Game Theory & Business Strategy***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2020

**Lecturer-in-Charge, *Business and Government***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2020

**Teaching Assistant, *Game Theory & Business Strategy***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2019–2020

**Lecturer, *Economics Perspectives***\
University of New South Wales
:::

::: {.cv-entry}
2017

**Teaching Assistant, *Advanced Industrial Organisation***\
University of Milan
:::

## Conferences & Invited Seminars {.cv-section}

American–European Health Economics Study Group · Invited seminar at CLEAN, Bocconi · STEP UP Conference ('What Works in Education?') · Australian Workshop on Public Finance · Labour Econometrics Workshop · Western Economic Association International Conference.

## Technical Skills {.cv-section}

R (incl. `arrow`, `duckdb`, `data.table` for larger-than-memory data) · Stata · Python · SQL.

## Other {.cv-section}

- Refereeing experience for *Empirical Economics* and *Health Policy & Technology*.
- Mentee at the *SOLE Mentoring Program* under Amanda Pallais (Harvard) and Basit Zafar (University of Michigan).

## References {.cv-section}

Available on request.
